query: Which top 3 entities had the most number of filings in FERC's rule making proceedings on large load interconnection in December 2025?
final_answer:
Assumption: I treated “entities” as **FERC eLibrary AUTHOR entities** in docket **RM26-4-000, Interconnection of Large Loads to the Interstate Transmission System**, and counted **distinct filings**, de-duplicating repeated author metadata within the same filing. Source: FERC eLibrary AdvancedSearch API query for **12/01/2025–12/31/2025**, keyword **“RM26-4”**, which returned **62** December 2025 filings in the docket; FERC eLibrary results include filed date, docket number, title, and affiliations.

### Result

There was **not a clean top 3**: only **one author entity filed more than once** in December 2025; nearly all other author entities were tied with **one filing**.

| Rank | Entity / author | December 2025 filings | Evidence |
|---:|---|---:|---|
| 1 | **Switch, Ltd.** | **2** | FERC eLibrary returned two RM26-4-000 records authored by Switch, Ltd.: “Reply Comments of Switch, Ltd…” filed **12/05/2025**, and “(doc-less) Motion to Intervene of Switch, Ltd. under RM26-4” filed **12/05/2025**. Source: FERC eLibrary AdvancedSearch API results for RM26-4, 12/01/2025–12/31/2025. |
| 2 | **Tie — all other author entities** | **1 each** | Examples include Advanced Energy Management Alliance, Google LLC, PJM Interconnection, L.L.C., Edison Electric Institute, Data Center Coalition, etc.; each appeared as author on one distinct December 2025 filing in the FERC eLibrary results. Source: same FERC eLibrary AdvancedSearch API result set. |
| 3 | **Tie — all other author entities** | **1 each** | Same tie group; no distinct third-place entity exists under a distinct-filing count. Source: same FERC eLibrary AdvancedSearch API result set. |

**Answer:** The only entity with more than one filing was **Switch, Ltd., with 2 filings**. Because all other author entities had **1 filing each**, there is **no unique top 3** beyond Switch; second and third place are tied among the remaining author entities.

python - <<'PY'
import pandas as pd, glob, os
for f in glob.glob('/work/ferc_search_*.csv'):
    print(f, os.path.getsize(f))
    print(pd.read_csv(f).head())
    print(pd.read_csv(f).columns)
PY

python - <<'PY'
import pandas as pd, glob, os
for f in glob.glob('/work/ferc_search_*.csv'):
    if os.path.getsize(f)<10: continue
    print(f, os.path.getsize(f))
    df=pd.read_csv(f)
    print(df.head().to_string())
    print(df.columns)
PY
